name: Terrain
description: A sophisticated endless procedural terrain generator using Perlin noise-based height maps with multi-layer texturing, object placement, and physics integration. Creates infinite explorable landscapes with height-based material blending (ditch, grass, rock, snow), dynamic chunk loading/unloading, and automatic object distribution. Supports GPU optimization, extensive texture customization, and seamless world generation.
author: stemstudio

isEndlessTerrain.name: Endless Terrain
isEndlessTerrain.description: When enabled, new terrain chunks are dynamically generated around the player as they move, creating an infinite explorable landscape.

useGPU.name: Use GPU Optimization
useGPU.description: Use WebGPU for optimized normal computation. Disable for compatibility with older browsers.

useEnhancedTerrain.name: Enhanced Terrain
useEnhancedTerrain.description: Enhanced terrain with smooth slopes, domain warping, and natural elevation zones. Disable for classic rolling hills.

waterEnabled.name: Water
waterEnabled.description: Enable water bodies in the terrain. When disabled, all terrain stays above sea level.

waterPercentage.name: Water Coverage %
waterPercentage.description: Percentage of terrain that is below water level. Higher values create more lakes and rivers.

maxHeight.name: Terrain Height
maxHeight.description: Maximum terrain elevation in world units. Controls the amplitude of the Perlin noise height map.

seed.name: Terrain Seed
seed.description: The seed value for the Perlin noise height generator. Different seeds produce different terrain layouts while keeping the same configuration.

grassMaxHeight.name: Grass Max Height
grassMaxHeight.description: Height where grass ends and rock begins

rockMaxHeight.name: Rock Max Height
rockMaxHeight.description: Height where rock ends and snow begins

ditchNormalTexture.name: Ditch Normal Map
ditchNormalTexture.description: Normal map for the ditch (low-lying) terrain layer, adding surface detail without extra geometry.

ditchRoughnessTexture.name: Ditch Roughness Map
ditchRoughnessTexture.description: Roughness map for the ditch terrain layer. Controls how shiny or matte the surface appears.

ditchUVScale.name: Ditch UV Scale
ditchUVScale.description: UV scale factor (0-1). Lower values stretch the texture.

ditchUVRepeat.name: Ditch Repeat Count
ditchUVRepeat.description: Exact number of repeats across the terrain on both axes.

groundTexture.name: Grass Texture
groundTexture.description: Color texture for the grass terrain layer, applied between sea level and Grass Max Height.

normalTexture.name: Grass Normal Map
normalTexture.description: Normal map for the grass terrain layer, adding surface detail without extra geometry.

grassRoughnessTexture.name: Grass Roughness Map
grassRoughnessTexture.description: Roughness map for the grass terrain layer. Controls how shiny or matte the surface appears.

grassUVScale.name: Grass UV Scale
grassUVScale.description: UV scale factor (0-1). Lower values stretch the texture.

grassUVRepeat.name: Grass Repeat Count
grassUVRepeat.description: Exact number of repeats across the terrain on both axes.

rockTexture.name: Rock Texture
rockTexture.description: Color texture for the rock terrain layer, applied between Grass Max Height and Rock Max Height.

rockNormalTexture.name: Rock Normal Map
rockNormalTexture.description: Normal map for the rock terrain layer, adding surface detail without extra geometry.

rockRoughnessTexture.name: Rock Roughness Map
rockRoughnessTexture.description: Roughness map for the rock terrain layer. Controls how shiny or matte the surface appears.

rockUVScale.name: Rock UV Scale
rockUVScale.description: UV scale factor (0-1). Lower values stretch the texture.

rockUVRepeat.name: Rock Repeat Count
rockUVRepeat.description: Exact number of repeats across the terrain on both axes.

snowNormalTexture.name: Snow Normal Map
snowNormalTexture.description: Normal map for the snow terrain layer, adding surface detail without extra geometry.

snowRoughnessTexture.name: Snow Roughness Map
snowRoughnessTexture.description: Roughness map for the snow terrain layer. Controls how shiny or matte the surface appears.

snowUVScale.name: Snow UV Scale
snowUVScale.description: UV scale factor (0-1). Lower values stretch the texture.

snowUVRepeat.name: Snow Repeat Count
snowUVRepeat.description: Exact number of repeats across the terrain on both axes.

treeDensity.name: Tree Density
treeDensity.description: Controls how densely trees are placed in forest areas. 0 = no trees, 50 = default, 100 = maximum density.

rockDensity.name: Rock Density
rockDensity.description: Controls how densely boulders are placed. 0 = no rocks, 50 = default, 100 = maximum density.

isDefaultState.name: Is Default State
isDefaultState.description: Tracks whether terrain objects are in default state (never customized by user)

modelAsset.name: Custom Model (Assets)
modelAsset.description: Select a model from your asset library to use instead of the bundled model

minScale.name: Min Scale
minScale.description: Minimum random scale multiplier for placed instances of this object.

maxScale.name: Max Scale
maxScale.description: Maximum random scale multiplier for placed instances of this object.

terrainOffset.name: Terrain Offset
terrainOffset.description: Vertical offset in world units (meters) applied to the object's base. Positive values sink the object into the terrain; negative values lift it above.

probability.name: Probability %
probability.description: Percentage chance (0-100) that this object spawns at each valid placement point.

objectType.name: Type
objectType.description: Category of terrain object, which determines height-based placement rules.

resetButton.name: Reset to Default
resetButton.description: Resets all terrain settings and objects back to their default values.

documentation:
## Terrain

A sophisticated endless procedural terrain generator using Perlin noise-based height maps with multi-layer texturing, object placement, and physics integration. Creates infinite explorable landscapes with height-based material blending (ditch, grass, rock, snow), dynamic chunk loading/unloading, and automatic object distribution. Supports GPU optimization, extensive texture customization, and seamless world generation.

### Key Features
- Infinite procedural terrain generation with chunk-based loading
- Perlin noise height function with configurable seed and amplitude
- Four terrain layers with height-based blending: ditch, grass, rock, snow
- Individual texture sets per layer: diffuse, normal, roughness maps
- Configurable UV scaling per texture layer (0-1 range)
- Optional WebGPU optimization for normal computation
- Automatic object placement: trees, rocks, grass, flowers, snow objects
- Height-based object distribution rules
- Physics mesh generation for terrain collision
- Chunk management with player-centric loading
- Editor preview support with live parameter updates
- Singleton behavior (one terrain per scene)
- Customizable terrain object types and densities
- Asset loading from library or custom models

### How It Works
1. Attach to plane/mesh object (becomes invisible terrain root)
2. Configure maxHeight (amplitude), seed, and height thresholds
3. Set texture URLs for each layer (ditch, grass, rock, snow)
4. On init, creates EndlessProceduralTerrain instance with height function
5. Height function: Perlin noise(x, z, seed) * maxHeight
6. Chunk system divides world into grid cells (chunkSize units)
7. As player/camera moves, chunks within view distance are generated
8. Each chunk:
   - Generates geometry via height sampling at chunkSegments resolution
   - Creates material with 4-layer shader (height-based blending)
   - Calculates normals (GPU-accelerated if enabled)
   - Applies textures with UV scaling
   - Generates physics trimesh for collision
9. Object placement system:
   - Samples terrain height at random positions
   - Places objects based on height thresholds and type rules
   - Instantiates from asset library or default models
   - Adds physics bodies for solid objects
10. Distant chunks unloaded to manage memory
11. Material blending:
    - Ditch: y < 0 (below sea level)
    - Grass: 0 < y < grassMaxHeight
    - Rock: grassMaxHeight < y < rockMaxHeight
    - Snow: y > rockMaxHeight
12. Editor mode: shows debug visualization, updates on attribute changes

### Common Use Cases
- Open-world games with large explorable areas
- Survival games needing infinite terrain
- Procedural generation for roguelike environments
- Flight simulators with ground terrain
- Exploration games with varied biomes
- Sandbox games with terrain modification
- Racing games with procedural tracks
- Nature simulation or ecosystem modeling
- Hiking or outdoor adventure games
- Sci-fi games with alien planet surfaces

### When Not To Use
- For handcrafted level design (use static meshes)
- In small enclosed environments (overkill)
- When precise terrain control is needed
- For 2D or flat-surface games
- When terrain should be destructible/modifiable at runtime
- For underwater environments (needs different approach)
- In VR with limited performance budget (heavy system)
- When multiple distinct terrain types in one scene
- For mobile platforms with severe performance constraints
- When terrain should follow specific map data or heightmaps

### Technical Details
- Chunk system: EndlessProceduralTerrain manages chunk lifecycle
- Height function: Perlin noise from EndlessTerrainHeight.getChunkSize()
- Chunk size: configurable via EndlessTerrainHeight (default varies)
- Chunk segments: 20 subdivisions per chunk for resolution
- Layer thresholds: grassMaxHeight (default 7), rockMaxHeight (default 12)
- GPU optimization: useGPU flag enables WebGPU normal calculation
- Texture loading: async via THREE.TextureLoader from URLs
- UV scaling: multiplies base UVs by scale factor (0-1)
- Material: custom shader with 4-layer height-based blending
- Physics: EndlessTerrainPhysics manages trimesh collision meshes
- Object placement: EndlessTerrainObjects with density-based distribution
- Object types: getDefaultTerrainObjectConfigs() provides default set
- Asset loading: assetLoader from game/editor for model imports
- Singleton enforcement: isSingleton: true in config
- Update loop: tracks player position, updates chunks, objects, physics
- Race condition protection: initializationToken prevents stale initializations
- Editor integration: onEditorAdded/AttributesUpdated for live preview
- Throttle priority: MEDIUM, throttling locked
- Backward compatibility: handles userData.isEndlessTerrain flag
- Performance: frustum culling disabled (chunks manage visibility)
